steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify conversion factors for area and time
steps.content:
To convert 180 cm²/hour to m²/minute, we need two conversions:
  • Area: 1 m = 100 cm, so 1 m² = 100² = 10,000 cm²
  • Time: 1 hour = 60 minutes

steps.title: Apply the conversions
steps.content:
Multiply the given rate by both conversion factors: $$180 \frac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{hour}} \times \frac{1 \text{ m}^2}{10,000 \text{ cm}^2} \times \frac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ min}} = \frac{180}{10,000 \times 60}$$
Simplify the fraction: $$\frac{180}{600,000} = \frac{3}{10,000} = 0.0003 \text{ m}^2/\text{min}$$
